title: Lamb Kebabs with Smoky Mediterranean Flair
description: Grilled lamb kebabs infused with the smoky flavors of the Mediterranean, perfect for a summer evening.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: You can also use lamb leg or a combination of lamb and beef.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality extra-virgin olive oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice is best.

ingredients.name: smoky paprika
ingredients.notes: This gives the kebabs their smoky flavor.

ingredients.name: garlic powder
ingredients.notes: Use a mild garlic powder to avoid overpowering the dish.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt or sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper is best.

ingredients.name: chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Use fresh parsley for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: chopped fresh mint
ingredients.notes: This adds a refreshing flavor to the kebabs.

ingredients.name: pita bread, for serving
ingredients.notes: Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it in the oven for a few minutes.

instructions: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat, about 400°F.
In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, smoky paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until they are cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Meanwhile, warm the pita breads by wrapping them in foil and heating them in the oven for a few minutes.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with chopped parsley and mint, and served with warm pita bread.
